spec: runs/lanes/trk_detbench_20260716/spec.md (incl. AMENDMENT 1, Track F manager, SKU fallback authorization)
status_meaning: Dispatch 2 (post-AMENDMENT-1) completed the full mission: VM provisioned (H100, amended ladder attempt 2), teardown rail VERIFIED armed before any work, arm 0a baseline reproduction PASSED at ~3e-11 (bar 0.0001), arm 0b feeder confound check flagged FEEDER_DRIFT (deltas >> 0.005, so all candidates are read paired-protocol vs 0b, both deltas reported), all four candidate arms ran and were scored with the frozen scorer + frozen association, incremental scoring after every arm, artifacts pulled two-sided, VM DELETED + list-confirmed. Diagnostic only; VERIFIED=0; no best_stack change; no promotion.

dispatch_1_history.outcome: NO_ATTEMPT (H100 SPOT stockout, 6/6 zones, $0, no orphans, ~24 min 16:03-16:27Z)

dispatch_2_provisioning.vm: pickleball-h100-detbench, a3-highgpu-1g SPOT, us-central1-a, pd-balanced 200GB from snapshot pickleball-fleet-snap-20260709-w7close, labels fable-lane=trk_detbench_20260716,fable-fleet=pickleball,owner=arnavchokshi

deimv2_dinov3_l_coco (HF model.safetenso.note: sha256 equals the HF LFS oid exactly; repo Intellindust-AI-Lab/DEIMv2 @ 0fff8d4dcdc272e6cf2d84be31399db471357941; NOT the _S_COCO artifact

arm_0a_reproduction.verdict: PASS (exact to float print precision) - harness and frozen association reproduce the frozen baseline on the VM

arm_0b_feeder_check.verdict: FEEDER_DRIFT (both clips, both metrics, far beyond 0.005). Per spec: candidate arms are interpreted paired-protocol vs 0b; deltas vs both 0a and 0b reported.
arm_0b_feeder_check.interpretation: The lane feeder (YOLO26m model.predict per-frame -> BOTSORT.update on the raw untrimmed pool) is NOT equivalent to the production pool path that produced the frozen trk_flip raw pools. The feeder pool admits more low-conf/spectator detections (cov4 jumps 0.71->0.97 on burlington but wolverine picks up 2 switches + 19 true-spectator FPs). Candidate detector rows therefore carry this same protocol offset, and cross-arm comparisons are only clean within the feeder protocol (0b vs arms 1-4).

verdicts.arm_0b: FEEDER_DRIFT flagged (not a candidate; the confound check did its job)
verdicts.arm_1_rfdetr_l: reject AS ZERO-SHOT DROP-IN (wolverine: 1 new switch + 16 spectator FP vs frozen baseline, cov4 -0.03 vs 0a / -0.20 vs paired 0b) BUT adopt-next-step AS THE FINE-TUNE BASE: burlington sweep (best IDF1 0.9204, cov4 0.9967, zero FP axes, and faster than the YOLO26m feeder 31.2 vs 34.6 ms/f), cleaner FP axes than its paired 0b baseline on wolverine (1<2 switches, 16<19 spectFP), Apache-2.0 vs incumbent AGPL
verdicts.arm_2_rfdetr_seg_l: reject (boxes): cov4 collapse both clips (0.66/0.39 - the predicted far-player recall regression at 504 native), 2 wolverine switches, detector wall ~7.4x baseline (>20% kill rule). Masks ARCHIVED (coco_rle, pulled) for the future mask-cue lane - that was this arm's other purpose
verdicts.arm_3_dfine_l: reject: burlington par-only with frozen baseline (0.8893/0.7100 vs 0.8831/0.7117), wolverine 1 new switch + cov4 0.5767 (worse than both baselines); no gate-relevant gain to trade
verdicts.arm_4_deimv2_l: reject: worst wolverine row of the bench (5 switches, 18 spectator FP, cov4 0.31) despite a good burlington row (0.9088/0.9117 clean) and fastest detector (21 ms/f); far-side small-player instability is disqualifying

fine_tune_decision_arm_go_no_go.recommendation: GO - RF-DETR-L is the fine-tune base
evidence: Ceiling exists: zero-shot RF-DETR-L burlington cov4 0.9967 crosses even the 0.95 promotion-bar level on that clip with zero switches/spectator/far FPs - the architecture can cover 4 players in this domain when players are resolvable
Speed is free: 31.2-31.8 ms/f batch-1 fp32 unoptimized is FASTER than the YOLO26m baseline feeder (34.6-38.1) - no runtime tax to pay for

The failure mode is domain, not architecture: every candidate AND the YOLO26m feeder itself degrade on wolverine's far-side small players + spectator-heavy pool; this is exactly what benchmark_spec_trk.md's fine-tune recipe (single on_court_player class + labeled spectator/passer hard negatives) targets
License: Apache-2.0 challenger vs AGPL-3.0 incumbent (NS-07.3 relicense pressure applies to the baseline itself)
Controls behaved: independent same-session controls (D-FINE-L, DEIMv2-L) did not beat RF-DETR-L, confirming the crosscheck ruling's rank-1 (refutation held)
cautions: Zero-shot RF-DETR-L is NOT adoptable today (wolverine gate axes fail vs the frozen baseline row) - the fine-tune must clear the pre-registered stop rule (two frozen-threshold attempts without material coverage gain, or ANY new switch/spectator/far FP -> stop the branch)
FEEDER_DRIFT means the fine-tune evaluation MUST route through the production pool path (or pin the paired 0b protocol) before any number is compared to the frozen baseline; do not mix protocols

rfdetr 1.8.3 emitted partial-load warnings (checkpoint lacks args.num_queries/group_detr -> flat-slice fallback; _kp_active_mask left random-init). Detections were empirically verified sane (boxes match production pool player boxes), but the fine-tune lane should pin the loader path and confirm these warnings are benign for training

runtime_summary_ms_per_frame_batch1.note: different native input sizes per detector (1536/704/504/640) - each model's designed operating point per the frozen protocol (no threshold/size grid allowed)

pulled_artifacts.contents: detbench_out/{raw,pools,scored,scores,masks} EXCLUDING per-arm reid_embeddings.json (large, non-load-bearing; precedent trk_reid_apron)
pulled_artifacts.full_manifest: runs/lanes/trk_detbench_20260716/vm_pull/detbench_ALL_SHA256.txt (112 files, sha256 of EVERYTHING incl. excluded embeddings)
pulled_artifacts.scores_also_pulled_loose: runs/lanes/trk_detbench_20260716/pulled/scores/s01..s06 (incremental after every arm - early results were safe from the start)
pulled_artifacts.mask_archive: arm2 RF-DETR-Seg-L per-frame coco_rle person masks, both clips, inside the tarball (detbench_out/masks/arm2_rfdetr_seg_l/*.masks.json)

teardown.instances_list_confirm: gcloud instances list (fleet filter) -> only historical pickleball-a100-fleet1 TERMINATED at delete time; name~pickleball-h100-detbench -> 0 items

fence_compliance.adapter_scripts: all in runs/lanes/trk_detbench_20260716/scripts/ (feeder_yolo26m_botsort_arm0b.py, candidate_detector_feeder.py, dump_rfdetr_raw.py, dump_dfine_raw.py, dump_deimv2_raw.py, vm_bootstrap.sh, provision_ladder_dispatch2.sh)

honest_issues: FEEDER_DRIFT is the biggest caveat on every candidate number: the lane feeder path (model.predict + BOTSORT.update on untrimmed pools) is measurably different from the production pool path (arm 0b vs 0a: burlington cov4 0.97 vs 0.71 from the SAME detector+tracker). All cross-arm readings in this card are clean only within the feeder protocol; absolute comparisons to the frozen baseline carry the protocol offset. Root cause not fully diagnosed this lane (candidates: production pre-role-lock trimming of the raw pool, decode path differences, model.track vs predict preprocessing).

rfdetr 1.8.3 load warnings (flat-slice fallback for group_detr; _kp_active_mask random-init) on BOTH rfdetr checkpoints - outputs empirically verified sane against known player boxes, but this is a package/checkpoint version-skew smell the fine-tune lane must pin down.

RF-DETR person class id: the package's class-name list is 0-indexed ('person' at 0) but the model emits 1-based COCO category ids - person is empirically id 1 (verified by box positions). Recorded because the mismatch would silently zero out detections if trusted naively.
DEIMv2 safetensors load had missing=2 keys (decoder.up, decoder.reg_scale - non-learned scale buffers left at module defaults); probe boxes were sane. unexpected=0.
Arm 2's mask archive stores per-frame per-detection masks UNLINKED to final track identities (the future mask-cue lane must do its own linking).
detector ms/frame numbers are fp32 batch-1 without optimize_for_inference() (rfdetr) / without TensorRT (all) - honest same-condition comparison, but absolute latencies are not deployment numbers.
arm0a detector wall is null in the runtime table because arm 0a consumed frozen pools (no detector ran - by design).

The reproduction pins matched to ~3e-11 rather than exactly 0.0 because the spec quotes 10-digit rounded values; the underlying scorer outputs are consistent with bitwise reproduction of the frozen preflip_score report.

vm_bootstrap.sh's repo autodiscovery (find -maxdepth 3) missed the snapshot's repo at depth 4 (~/coldstart_20260706/repo); located manually, no impact. The snapshot repo was at e64865cf before the bundle pin was applied; bundle checkout to cabdb8cf succeeded cleanly.

Dispatch-1 stockout history preserved: the first 6-attempt H100-only ladder no-attempted out same day ($0); AMENDMENT 1 authorized the SKU fallback ladder, and H100 landed on amended attempt 2 (us-central1-a) - the A100 tiers were never needed.
